strategic_profile.executive_summary: The University of Arkansas is a public land-grant research university and the flagship campus of the University of Arkansas System [cite: 1]. Recognized as an R1 institution, it is highly regarded for its Sam M. Walton College of Business, strong supply chain management programs, and robust Honors College [cite: 2, 3]. The university offers a vibrant SEC campus life heavily influenced by Greek organizations and benefits from deep ties to regional corporate giants like Walmart and Tyson Foods [cite: 4, 5].
strategic_profile.admissions_philosophy: Holistic review for test-optional or borderline applicants, but largely numbers-focused for students meeting automatic admission thresholds [cite: 6, 7]

analyst_takeaways.insight: While overall acceptance remains high (74.3%), out-of-state competition is increasing due to aggressive regional tuition discount programs [cite: 8, 9].
analyst_takeaways.implication: Applicants from bordering states view UArk as a highly viable alternative to their own more expensive or hyper-competitive in-state flagships (e.g., Texas) [cite: 9].

analyst_takeaways.insight: The New Arkansan Non-Resident Tuition Award (NRTA) automatically grants heavily discounted tuition (paying 80-90% of the difference) to students from neighboring states with a 3.2+ GPA [cite: 10, 11].
analyst_takeaways.implication: It is an extremely cost-effective option for students in Texas, Louisiana, Missouri, and Oklahoma, making it functionally similar to in-state tuition for high achievers [cite: 9, 11].

analyst_takeaways.insight: Greek life and SEC sports (Razorback football) dominate the social scene, which can feel alienating to students not involved in those ecosystems [cite: 4].
analyst_takeaways.implication: Students seeking a traditional, spirited Southern collegiate experience will thrive, but those looking for smaller, independent social scenes must actively seek out niches [cite: 4].

campus_dynamics.social_environment: A large, spirited campus characterized by strong Greek life, pervasive SEC sports culture, and an outdoorsy vibe given its location in the Ozark Mountains [cite: 4].
campus_dynamics.transportation_impact: Located in Fayetteville with excellent access to outdoor recreation, though on-campus parking shortages are a frequently cited concern among students [cite: 12, 13].
campus_dynamics.research_impact: An R1 institution with over $184 million in research expenditures, offering significant undergraduate research opportunities, particularly through the heavily endowed Honors College [cite: 14, 15].

current_status.test_policy_details: Test-optional for applicants with a 3.20+ high school GPA. Test scores are still required for most merit-based scholarships, state aid, and enrollment/placement purposes [cite: 6, 16].

longitudinal_trends.notes: Waitlist acceptance surged dramatically in 2024 compared to previous cycles [cite: 17, 18].
Acceptance rate from Clastify historical data [cite: 17, 19].

gpa.notes: GPA is recalculated on a 4.0 scale weighting only AP, IB, and dual enrollment courses [cite: 6, 14].

testing.policy_note: Submission rate reflects ACT submissions; SAT submission rate is approximately 31% [cite: 8, 20].

colleges.name: Sam M. Walton College of Business
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Ideal for students seeking high ROI in Supply Chain Management or Retail, leveraging proximity to Walmart and Tyson Foods [cite: 5, 21].

majors.name: Business Administration, Management and Operations
majors.special_requirements: Students in Business face a differential tuition rate ($365.15/credit vs standard $269.75) [cite: 22].
majors.notes: The most popular major at the university by graduate volume [cite: 23].

colleges.name: College of Engineering
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Students interested in heavily-recruited technical fields. In-state students seeking top-tier regional engineering education [cite: 21].

majors.name: Computer Science
majors.special_requirements: B.S. requires a two-semester capstone project [cite: 24].
notable_professors: Dr. Jia Di (Asynchronous integrated circuit design, Hardware security)
majors.notes: Students without prior coding knowledge can succeed, though supplemental projects are heavily recommended to match elite school employment outcomes [cite: 25, 26].

colleges.name: College of Education and Health Professions
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Students pursuing nursing or health education [cite: 2].

majors.name: Nursing
majors.special_requirements: Nursing is a capped program requiring specific secondary applications or alternate major designation [cite: 27].
majors.notes: One of the most competitive programs on campus [cite: 27].

colleges.name: Fay Jones School of Architecture and Design
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Students highly dedicated to architecture; undeclared status is not an option here [cite: 28].

application_deadlines.notes: Priority admissions deadline. Scholarship priority deadline is Nov 15 [cite: 29].

supplemental_requirements.details: High-value fellowships (Bodenhamer, Sturgis) require extensive supplemental applications and essays emphasizing public service, leadership, and intellectual curiosity [cite: 31, 32].

holistic_factors.specific_differentiators: Admissions are highly metric-driven. Essays and interviews are not part of general admissions but are critical for competitive fellowships [cite: 31, 33].

major_selection_tactics: Apply Priority (Nov 1) as capacity for popular programs can fill quickly and waitlists are utilized heavily late in the cycle [cite: 6, 34].
Apply directly to the major if interested in Architecture or Nursing, as these are capped/impacted [cite: 27].
application_strategy.alternate_major_strategy: The university considers alternate majors if a student is rejected from capped majors like Nursing or Architecture [cite: 27]. Choosing an undeclared major is permitted for Arts/Sciences and Business, but not for Architecture [cite: 28].

financials.tuition_model: Public, with massive regional discounting via the NRTA

scholarships.name: New Arkansan Non-Resident Tuition Award (NRTA)
scholarships.benefits: Massively reduces out-of-state tuition for students from TX, LA, MS, TN, MO, KS, and OK with a 3.2+ GPA [cite: 10, 11].

scholarships.name: Bodenhamer Fellowship
scholarships.benefits: Provides $80,000 over 4 years; covers tuition, room, board, and funds study abroad/research. Requires 32 ACT / 1430 SAT and 3.9 GPA [cite: 31, 35].

scholarships.name: Honors College Fellowship
scholarships.benefits: Provides $80,000 over 4 years. Given to 70-80 students per year. Requires 32 ACT / 1430 SAT and 3.9 GPA [cite: 31, 35].

scholarships.name: Chancellor's Merit Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Awarded to National Merit Finalists naming UArk as their first choice. Grants out-of-state students in-state tuition [cite: 36].

scholarships.name: Alumni Legacy Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: For qualified non-resident undergraduate students who have a legacy connection that is a current member of the Alumni Association [cite: 37].

ap_policy.general_rule: Score directly determines credit, unless an exemption is given or department review is needed for borderline scores [cite: 38].
ap_policy.usage: Credits are entered as 'CR' and can be used to skip prerequisites, but do not satisfy minimum residency requirements [cite: 39].

ib_policy.general_rule: Grants credit for specific IB exam scores. All IB exams, online or onsite, are accepted [cite: 39, 40].

transfer_articulation.restrictions: Students must complete a minimum of 30 upper-level hours at the Fayetteville campus to earn a degree [cite: 41].

what_it_takes: ACT scores above 24 required for most meaningful merit aid [cite: 11].
Top 1-2% of applicants (3.9+ GPA, 32+ ACT) needed for elite fellowships [cite: 35].
essay_tips: General admissions do not require essays [cite: 42].
For fellowships: Show yourself in action, keep writing succinct, and use specific numbers/sources [cite: 32].
red_flags: Applying after the Nov 1 priority deadline if expecting housing and top scholarships [cite: 6].
insights: The campus is seen as a major draw for Texas students looking to escape the hyper-competitive admissions at UT/A&M while paying functionally identical tuition [cite: 9].
Parking is notoriously difficult and heavily cited in negative reviews [cite: 12, 13].
Computer Science is strong but requires extensive self-directed projects/internships to match elite school outcomes [cite: 25].
